name: Mere Humnafas
description: Mere Humnafas is an intimate, immersive musical–storytelling experience that reconnects contemporary audiences with the emotional and cultural roots of the subcontinent’s rich poetic and musical traditions. At its core lies a simple yet powerful idea: to create something truly new, we must first engage deeply with the masters who shaped our artistic language.For generations, we’ve been reminded that innovation doesn’t exist in isolation—it grows through dialogue with history, memory, and those who came before us. Mere Humnafas brings this philosophy to life by revisiting timeless ghazals, nazms, and poetic expressions that have shaped our collective emotional landscape. These classics are reimagined through contemporary acoustic arrangements, featuring only a keyboard and acoustic guitar, allowing the poetry and emotion to take center stage with striking intimacy.Woven through the music is a narrative that explores enduring themes of love, longing, separation, lost affection, and unspoken devotion—emotions that remain deeply relevant today. Through spoken word and reflection, the experience bridges generations, offering a modern lens while honoring the depth and beauty of the original voices.Mere Humnafas invites you into a dreamy, almost surreal evening—an hour where past and present dissolve into one, where memory and imagination intertwine, and where music and poetry remind us of the power of feeling deeply.This is not just a performance.It is a return—to our roots, our stories, and to each other.

name: Krishna Nāda - An Evening of Rāga & Bhakti
description: Krishna Nāda – An Evening of Rāga & Bhakti 🪔🎶Step into an atmosphere where music transcends performance and becomes a path to stillness, devotion, and inner connection. Krishna Nāda is a thoughtfully curated classical musical evening that brings together the richness of Indian tradition with a deeply spiritual experience.Set within the serene and divine surroundings of ISKCON Temple Dwarka, this evening invites you to pause, reflect, and immerse yourself in the timeless beauty of Rāga, Bhajan, and Naam Sankirtan.✨ What Makes This Experience Special• Two Live Classical & Devotional Performances– Vocal Recital: A soulful expression of Hindustani classical and devotional music, rich in bhaav and tradition– Santoor Recital: A meditative instrumental journey accompanied by tabla and pakhawaj• A Curated Spiritual FlowFrom Raaga invocation to collective chanting, the evening is designed to gradually elevate your inner state - from sound to stillness• Sacred Venue ExperienceHosted at ISKCON Dwarka, offering a divine ambience, spiritual energy, and an elevated cultural setting• Beyond MusicThis is not just a concert = it is a space for connection, reflection, and shared devotion• Followed by Live Snacks Prasadam 🍽Conclude your evening with a warm, community-driven dining experience📍 Event DetailsLocation: ISKCON Temple, Sector 13, Dwarka, New DelhiDate: Sunday, 10th May 2026Time: 6:00 PM onwards🎟 Entry: ₹1700Limited seats for an intimate and premium experience

name: Guzaarish
description: Kathika in collaboration with Vrindan invites you all to experience Guzaarish, an evocative musical experience that weaves together the soulful strands of thumri, ghazal, raga and rhythm into a richly layered soundscape. Inspired by the intimate acoustics of traditional havelis, where music once echoed through courtyards and corridors, this concert brings alive a timeless era of expression and artistry.Presented by Vrindan and performed by internationally acclaimed artists, Guzaarish is not just a concert, but a living, breathing exchange between performers and audience. Through a unique request-led format, the evening unfolds organically, allowing listeners to shape the journey and reconnect with melodies that hold personal meaning.Rooted in the golden era of semi-classical music and infused with the charm of vintage cinema, each rendition carries nostalgia, poetic depth and emotional resonance. From beloved classics to hidden gems, Guzaarish invites you to rediscover, reminisce, and revel in a shared musical journey, one that is as intimate as it is unforgettable.

description: Step into a powerful inner journey where sound becomes a pathway for deep healing.This 108 Mahamrityunjaya Mantra experience is designed as a deep energetic process — working beyond the surface of the mind, into the subtle layers of your body and consciousness.Through guided breath, rhythmic repetition, and sound, the vibration begins to move through your system — gently releasing stored stress, emotional heaviness, and energetic blockages. As the layers begin to soften, a natural state of calm, clarity, and inner healing emerges.This experience supports:✨ Emotional release and energetic healing✨ Deep nervous system relaxation✨ Letting go of fear, control, and inner resistance✨ Reconnecting with a more centred, grounded stateThe Mahamrityunjaya mantra carries a deeper significance of release from inner limitations and restoration of balance withinWith 108 repetitions, the sound creates a meditative rhythm that aligns breath, body, and awareness — allowing healing to unfold naturally, without force.

name: Earth & Rhythm: Drum Circle
description: The Earth has its own music… have you been listening?Step into a space where rhythm becomes connection, and sound becomes a shared experience. Rhythm & Earth is not just a drum circle—it’s a guided, immersive journey into collective energy, expression, and presence.In this 60-minute experience, you’ll enter a circle of like-minded individuals and co-create rhythms together—no prior musical experience needed. As the beats align, something shifts: barriers soften, energy opens, and a natural sense of harmony begins to flow.This is where you don’t just play music… you feel it.Each session is intuitively guided yet organically shaped by the group, making every circle a unique and living experience. Whether you come to unwind, connect, or simply explore something new, this is your invitation to drop into rhythm and return to yourself.

name: Correlation Is Not Causation
description: Join us for a lecture that begins with a deceptively simple question:When two things move together, does one actually cause the other?About the LectureWe live in a world overflowing with data.Patterns are everywhere — ice cream sales rise, and so do drowning rates. But does one cause the other? Or are we just seeing coincidence dressed up as meaning?This lecture unpacks why economists insist, almost stubbornly, that correlation is not causation — and why that distinction matters far beyond the classroom.At the heart of economics and public policy lies a deeper question: how do we know if something actually works?The session explores:The difference between correlation and causation, and why it’s easy to confuse the twoHow economists use tools like hypothesis testing and p-values to separate signal from noiseWhat methods like difference-in-differences and regression discontinuity reveal about real-world impactHow policies are evaluated — from taxes to welfare programs — to determine whether they truly change outcomesThrough intuitive examples, the lecture moves beyond technical jargon to show how causal thinking shapes decisions that affect everyday life.About the SpeakerParush Arora is an Assistant Professor of Economics at Ashoka University. He completed his PhD in Economics from the University of California Irvine.His research focuses on econometrics, Bayesian inference, and empirical macroeconomics — fields that sit at the intersection of data, uncertainty, and decision-making. He holds a Bachelor’s degree in Economics from the University of Delhi and a Master’s from the Madras School of Economics.About unLectureunLecture brings experts out of classrooms and into cafés, bars and neighbourhood spaces across Delhi. Created by three friends from college who wanted learning to feel warm, social and alive. unLecture turns an otherwise ordinary evening into a conversation you may think about long after it ends.

name: The Morning Table: A Sunday Breakfast Gathering
description: The Brine Club is our way of bringing people together over food that’s meant to be eaten slowly — and building a community of like-minded people around the table.Every table is different, every menu changes — because I like letting the day decide where things go.Most of our gatherings are sit-down.The Morning Table is a Sunday breakfast brunch hosted at our home in Gurgaon. ( Exact location to be shared 24 hours before the event to maintain privacy)We’re putting together a menu of breakfast dishes from different parts of the world — the kind you usually only see online or in other cities. Think London’s viral honey caramel butter toast, fried chicken & waffles, and our own slightly indulgent mango brioche toast, along with a few other plates.One beverage of your choice is included. If you feel like another round, additional drinks can be ordered during the brunch on an à la carte basis.Everything will be served family-style, so the table eats together, passes plates around.The full menu is in the gallery.You’ll be sharing the table with 10 people — some strangers, some soon-to-be friends. If you’re someone who enjoys meeting new, interesting people over really good food, this might be your kind of Sunday morning.It’s relaxed, a little indulgent, and not the kind of breakfast you usually find around the city.

name: A summer table from Odisha! - Pakhala Pop Up
description: Pakhala Pop-Up: A Summer Table from OdishaA Rosei Ghara x Kartik’s Kitchen CollaborationWe bring to the table a ritual from coastal Odisha — a meal designed for summer.Pakhala is not just fermented rice. It is comfort, cooling, seasonal wisdom, and memory served in a bowl.On the 26th of April, from 12 pm onwards, Rosei Ghara, in collaboration with Kartik’s Kitchen, invites you to experience an Odia summer table — thoughtfully curated, slow, and rooted in tradition.What to Expect:At the centre sits Pakhala — lightly fermented rice, cooling and probiotic-rich — accompanied by an abundant spread of classic summer sides. Expect crisp fries, smoky mash, leafy greens, lentil badis, seasonal vegetables, and carefully spiced accompaniments — each dish balancing texture, heat, tang, and crunch.Why It’s Special:• A rare opportunity to experience Odisha’s iconic summer meal in Gurgaon• A collaboration between two kitchens that celebrate regional Indian flavours• Limited seating to preserve the intimacy of the table• Hosted in K-38 Studios, DLF PHASE I, Gurgaon• A slow, immersive lunch designed around seasonality and traditionPakhala is typically eaten for lunch in Odia homes — and we invite you to experience it exactly as it is meant to be enjoyed: unhurried, generous, and shared.📍 Venue: K-38 Studios, Gurgaon🗓️ Date : 26th April🕛 Time: 12 pm onwards✨ Limited seats | Community dining experienceCome curious. Come hungry.Leave with a new understanding of how Odisha does summer.

name: Nizamuddin Heritage walk on Sufism & Poetry
description: In the Sufi Bylanes of Nizamuddin — A Heritage Walk of Stories, Shrines & SilenceStep into the tender, time-worn lanes of Nizamuddin, where the fragrance of incense mingles with qawwali echoes and centuries of faith, love, and longing still breathe through stone and sky.Join us for a soulful heritage walk through the sacred precincts of Nizamuddin Dargah, as we trace the footsteps of saints, poets, patrons, and wanderers who made this neighbourhood a living archive of Delhi’s Sufi soul.On this journey, we will also pause at:✨ Ghalib’s Mazar — remembering the poet of longing and lament✨ Chausath Khamba — a marble pavilion of quiet grace and symmetry✨ Atagha Khan’s Tomb — a Mughal-era resting place of power and piety✨ Pateh Shah’s Dargah — where devotion meets deep serenity    & end at the Nizammuddin Dargah.Along the way, we’ll listen to stories of shrines and seekers, of migration and memory, of music, miracles, and mysticism — stories that still whisper through these alleys at dusk.

location.address: Bharat Scouts and Guides Marg, opposite Humayun Tomb Park, Nizamuddin, Hazrat Nizamuddin, National Zoological Park, Sundar Nagar, New Delhi, Delhi 110013, India

name: 1857 & Dilli : A heritage walk in Delhi Ridge
description: 1857 UPRISING & DILLI: (A heritage walk at the Mutiny Memorial & it's neighbourhood in Kamla Nehru Ridge )The year 1857 marked a turning point in Indian history. What began as a sepoy mutiny in Meerut soon transformed into a widespread rebellion against British colonial rule. Within days, the rebelling sepoys marched to Delhi, a city symbolic of imperial power, where they declared the aging Mughal emperor Bahadur Shah Zafar as the leader of the uprising.The Mutiny Memorial, now known as Ajitgarh, is a prominent monument located on the Delhi Ridge, near Kashmere Gate. It was constructed in 1863 by the British to honor their soldiers and officials who died during the 1857 uprising.Let’s explore the stories of the 1857 uprising and its impacts, at the Mutiny Memorial (a haunted site ) &  its neighborhood landmarks (Ashokan Pillar, Baoli, Pir Ghaib Hunting Lodge & the Hindu Rao Hospital)

location.address: Kamla Nehru Ridge, Civil Lines, Delhi, 110007, India

name: The World Of Work In Contemporary India
description: Join us for a lecture that asks a question that feels increasingly urgent:Why does economic growth no longer guarantee jobs?About the LectureAcross the world — and especially in India — stories of job loss, retrenchment, and precarious work have become almost routine.But these are not isolated disruptions. They are part of a deeper structural shift.This lecture examines the changing world of work through the lens of neoliberal capitalism — a phase that began gaining global momentum in the late 20th century and has since reshaped economies, institutions, and livelihoods. It explores how processes such as extractivism, land acquisition, resource grabbing, and intensified profit-seeking have transformed not just production, but the very conditions under which labour exists.The session explores:Why economic growth has become increasingly “jobless”The declining relationship between output and employment across sectorsHow neoliberal policy regimes reshape labour markets and livelihoodsWhat Marxian political economy reveals about accumulation and labour displacementDrawing on the idea of the “Absolute General Law of Capitalist Accumulation,” the lecture argues that labour displacement is not accidental, but intrinsic to the logic of capital itself,  driven by the constant expansion of productive forces and the pursuit of accumulation.At its core, this talk invites us to rethink what we call a “crisis” — not as a temporary breakdown, but as a systemic feature of how contemporary capitalism functions.About the SpeakerPraveen Jha is former Sukhamoy Chakravarty Chair Professor of Economics at Jawaharlal Nehru University, where he taught at the Centre for Economic Studies and Planning for several decades.His work spans political economy of development, with a focus on labour, agriculture, and education, alongside interests in natural resources, public finance, and the history of economic thought. He has also been affiliated with the Centre for Informal Sector and Labour Studies at JNU and has held visiting positions at institutions across Germany, China, South Africa, Zimbabwe, and Switzerland, including engagements with organisations such as the International Labour Organization.He has been associated with multiple research initiatives with UN agencies including the ILO, UNICEF, UNDP, and FAO, and is a founding editor of Agrarian South: Journal of Political Economy. With over twenty books and more than a hundred academic publications, his work remains central to debates on development, labour, and capitalism in India and beyond.About unLectureunLecture brings experts out of classrooms and into cafés, bars and neighbourhood spaces across Delhi. Created by three friends from college who wanted learning to feel warm, social and alive. unLecture turns an otherwise ordinary evening into a conversation you may think about long after it ends.

location.address: E-17, Market Ln, Hauz Khas Market, Block E, Hauz Khas, Delhi, New Delhi, Delhi 110016, India

name: 26th April: Blueprints for Anti-Caste Protagonism
description: Join us for a lecture that asks a difficult question:Why is it so hard to see anti-caste assertion, even when it is right in front of us?About the LectureWhat does it mean to represent a body that history has refused to recognise?This lecture turns to art as a site of both inquiry and resistance, examining how caste operates not only through direct violence, but through subtler, representational forms that shape what can be seen, known, and acknowledged.Drawing from works such as Mukta Salve Triptych (2023) and Tannerfilm (2024), the session traces how infrastructures of caste have historically made it nearly impossible for anti-caste voices and assertions to be positively registered. It examines the lingering continuities between colonial ethnography of the 1850s and contemporary modes of representation — both of which render the Dalit body as devoid of agency, even in moments of clear self-assertion.The talk explores:How caste operates through visual and cultural representationWhy anti-caste assertion is often rendered illegible or erasedThe continuity between colonial and contemporary ways of seeing Dalit subjectivityHow art can function as a space of critique, interruption, and re-imaginationAbout the SpeakerRahee Punyashloka is an artist, writer, and experimental filmmaker based between Bhubaneswar and New Delhi. His work spans visual art, film, and writing, with a focus on creating anti-caste imagery and emancipatory imaginaries for Dalit subjectivity.His works have been exhibited at institutions and festivals including Khoj Studios, the Museum of Art and Photography, Haus der Kulturen der Welt, the Tribeca Film Festival, and the International Film Festival Rotterdam. He is also known by his online moniker artedkar.All Grounds for Thought tickets include a ₹250 coupon redeemable on food and beverages at the venue — so you can listen, reflect, and stay for conversation.About unLectureunLecture brings experts out of classrooms and into cafés, bars and neighbourhood spaces across Delhi. Created by three friends from college who want learning to feel warm, social and alive. unLecture turns an otherwise ordinary evening into a conversation you may think about long after it ends.

name: The Procrastireaders : Books & Coffee Tasting
description: The Procrastireaders: Books & Coffee TastingOverviewIf you have been wanting to get back to reading but find it hard to make the time at home, this is for you. The Procrastireaders is a dedicated space to finally open that book you have been meaning to finish. It is a slow paced morning designed for quiet reading and great coffee in the company of others who are doing the same.What is Stories of Hope?We are a community led initiative dedicated to creating safe and mindful spaces. Through intimate workshops and gatherings, we help individuals navigate life’s complexities from mental well being to personal growth with empathy and shared human connection.What to expectBring Your Own Book: Whether it is a novel you started months ago or a new non fiction find, bring along whatever you are currently reading.Dedicated Reading Time: Enjoy a block of quiet, uninterrupted time to sink into your book without the distractions of chores or your phone.Shared Atmosphere: The comfort of reading alongside a small group of people who are also prioritizing their love for books.Coffee & Chat: A low pressure chance to talk about what you are reading (or why you have been procrastinating) over a coffee at Unlocked.Details🗓️ Date: 25th April (Saturday)⏰ Time: 11:30 AM📍 Location: Unlocked, 32nd Avenue🎟️ Contribution: ₹700 (Includes Coffee)

name: ALT EFF Film Club x GuruJal Gurugram | 15th May
description: If you are tired of the outrage cycle, doomscrolling collapse, and the looming AI takeover narrative. If you have caught yourself going numb just to cope. We see you.All Living Things Environmental Film Festival is back with the third season of Film Club. A monthly meet up across 10 cities over 5 months, orbiting environmental cinema that cracks you open without trapping you in doom and gloom.It is a room full of people choosing to show up in cities whose infrastructure persuades you to stay home, choosing to sit together, watch together, and turn private anxiety into collective imagination. Messy. Human. Honest conversation.With snacks. Always snacks.To become a Film Club Member, continue with the regular booking process. The membership option will appear after you select the venue and date.All activities, including discussions and refreshments, will occur between 7:00 and 9:00 PM.What's Included?Film screeningGroup discussionLight refreshmentsCommunity spaceStudent Discount50% off for students with a valid ID (& must bring ID to the screening).CODE: ALTSTUDENTABOUT ALT EFFAll Living Things Environmental Film Festival (ALT EFF) is the world’s largest decentralised film festival and a premiere showcase for environmental cinema.Across its annual festival, monthly Film Club, schools initiatives, film fund, and year round curations, ALT EFF nurtures stories grounded in care, collective responsibility, stewardship, and an abiding love for the natural world.Spanning countries, genres, languages, and forms, it brings together stories reimagining our relationship with all living things.

location.address: 6/9, F-2, Block F, DLF Phase 1, Sector 26A, Gurugram, Haryana 122002, India
